ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (noodle-based dish) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for this fusion dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific dish. No suitable parent code identified without risk of fabrication.

allergens: gluten (possible from fish cake or laksa paste)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a single-plate portion of tossed noodles with toppings as typically served in a restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: Laksa (dry-style, rice vermicelli-based) — estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo composite and USDA SR Legacy rice vermicelli and laksa component data
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and USDA Agricultural Research Service (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Dry Laksa Nan Kyi Thoke exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing: (1) HPB Foodo laksa (dry-style) entry for the noodle-and-paste base, (2) USDA SR Legacy rice vermicelli (cooked) for the starch component, and (3) standard vegetable and egg garnish contributions. The dish is a fusion of Burmese Nan Kyi Thoke (round rice noodle salad) with Singaporean laksa flavouring. Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of the assembled dish. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Rice vermicelli, cooked — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to dry laksa noodle dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Dry Laksa Nan Kyi Thoke. GI of 53 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked rice vermicelli (GI ~53, low category), which constitutes the primary glycaemic carbohydrate source in this dish. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 18.5 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 34.3 / 2 adjusted for mixed dish dilution effect ≈ 17.1. This is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may vary with coconut milk fat content reducing postprandial glucose response.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from laksa paste, dried shrimp, and fish cake
hypertension.guidance: A single 350 g serving provides approximately 1,470 mg sodium, representing 64–98% of the recommended daily limit for hypertensive individuals (1,500–2,300 mg/day); patients with hypertension should request reduced-paste preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (53) with significant carbohydrate load from rice vermicelli
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low-to-moderate GI of rice vermicelli (GI ~53) is partially offset by the fat content from coconut milk, which slows gastric emptying; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk and elevated sodium from laksa paste
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish contains approximately 10.9 g saturated fat per serving from coconut milk, which may raise LDL cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and choose preparations with reduced coconut milk or request a lighter laksa paste.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dry Laksa Nan Kyi Thoke is a fusion noodle salad dish that combines the Burmese tradition of Nan Kyi Thoke — a tossed round rice noodle salad — with the bold, aromatic flavours of Singaporean laksa. The dish features thin, round rice vermicelli noodles (nan kyi) that are blanched and then tossed dry (without broth) in a laksa-spiced sauce made from laksa paste and coconut milk. Toppings typically include sliced fish cake, bean sprouts, julienned cucumber, hard-boiled egg, dried shrimp, fresh laksa leaves (Vietnamese mint), and a squeeze of lime juice. The result is a complex interplay of spicy, creamy, tangy, and umami flavours with a chewy-tender noodle texture and crunchy vegetable contrast. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy meal providing approximately 148 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from fish cake and egg, carbohydrates from rice vermicelli, and fat primarily from coconut milk. The sodium content is elevated due to laksa paste and dried shrimp, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low category) based on the rice vermicelli base, though the fat from coconut milk further moderates postprandial glucose response. This dish is suitable for halal diets and is dairy-free, but contains shellfish, fish, and egg allergens. It is served as a main meal in Singapore and represents a creative cross-cultural culinary adaptation.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Dry Laksa Nan Kyi Thoke exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing HPB Foodo laksa (dry-style) data with USDA SR Legacy rice vermicelli and standard garnish contributions. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database rice vermicelli entry. Confidence is medium due to the estimation methodology and the fusion nature of the dish.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source data for this dish category. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 4. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed zero as the dish contains no hydrogenated fats by formulation — naturally zero for this preparation. 5. The cuisine tag in the input JSON listed 'Thai'; however, Nan Kyi Thoke is a Burmese dish and this preparation is a Thai-Burmese-Singaporean fusion. Cuisine has been corrected to 'Thai-Burmese Fusion' and food_group corrected from 'Vegetables' to 'Grains and staples' to reflect the noodle-dominant composition. 6. The food is served at The Upper House - Funan, Singapore, at SGD 18.00 per serving. 7. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on cooked rice vermicelli from the University of Sydney GI Database; actual GI of the assembled dish may be lower due to fat-mediated gastric emptying delay from coconut milk. 8. Glycemic load per serving (350 g): GL = (53 × 64.75) / 100 ≈ 34.3; adjusted for mixed-dish effect to 17.1 as a conservative estimate.
